title: update_space_fully replacing update_space_props + apply_member_patch is the right structural fix
description: 18000ea removed update_space_props and apply_member_patch and replaced them with update_space_fully, which wraps all writes in a single SQLite transaction. The two-function design was the structural cause of the atomicity gap: update_space_props committed its writes before apply_member_patch ran, so a server error on the member write left the name change persisted with notUpdated returned. The single-transaction design closes this gap at the architecture level rather than patching around it. Resist any suggestion to restore the two functions for 'simplicity' or reuse — the gap they created was a documented known limitation for one commit (ecb97a8) precisely because the two-function design couldn't solve it.
